raw:
{
  "judge_id": "J123",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.4": 5,
    "R1.7": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "The agent output maps against the nearest works (Sheridan, Bemis & Murcko) and identifies sharp deltas between them and the proposed RigorDTI benchmark.",
    "R1.7": "The agent output audits every load-bearing citation and flags missing key works with bibliographic precision (e.g., correcting the Sheridan et al. 2024 citation).",
    "RX.1": "For each recommendation, the agent marks confidence and missing evidence (e.g., uncertainty about the intended Sheridan reference and scaffold-split numbers).",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed active set J*(d) without any drift.",
    "RX.4": "No regressions or harmful content are introduced in the revised draft; instead, the agent identifies and corrects errors (e.g., MSE vs. RMSE inconsistency).",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason (e.g., citation correction, dispersion arithmetic correction)."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.4": 0.9,
    "R1.7": 0.95,
    "RX.1": 0.85,
    "RX.3": 0.9,
    "RX.4": 0.95,
    "RX.5": 0.9
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.4: The agent output maps against the nearest works (Sheridan, Bemis & Murcko) and identifies sharp deltas between them and the proposed RigorDTI benchmark.
rationale.R1.7: The agent output audits every load-bearing citation and flags missing key works with bibliographic precision (e.g., correcting the Sheridan et al. 2024 citation).
rationale.RX.1: For each recommendation, the agent marks confidence and missing evidence (e.g., uncertainty about the intended Sheridan reference and scaffold-split numbers).
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed active set J*(d) without any drift.
rationale.RX.4: No regressions or harmful content are introduced in the revised draft; instead, the agent identifies and corrects errors (e.g., MSE vs. RMSE inconsistency).
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason (e.g., citation correction, dispersion arithmetic correction).
